research.interface: ServiceNow REST Table API, /api/now/table (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of GET/POST/PATCH /api/now/table/{tableName}, the sysparm_query/sysparm_fields/sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset parameters, the {"result": ...} response envelope, and the Link and X-Total-Count pagination headers

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full method and parameter tables: sysparm_limit default 10000, sysparm_offset default 0, display-value/reference-link/no-count/suppress-pagination parameters, Accept/Content-Type requirements, status codes 200/201/204/400/404, Location header on POST

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow publishes no official Python SDK for the Table API; the documented integration surface is plain REST over /api/now/table, and Python's stdlib urllib is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to call it from scheduled sync jobs.

verified_facts: The Table API exposes GET (list), GET/{sys_id}, POST, PATCH, PUT and DELETE under /api/now/table/{tableName}, with an optional versioned form /api/now/{api_version}/table/{tableName}; the unversioned path serves the latest version.

List queries are filtered with sysparm_query (encoded query: '^' conjunctions, '<field>IN<values>' membership, '^ORDERBY<field>' ordering), projected with sysparm_fields (comma-separated), and paginated with sysparm_limit (default 10000) plus sysparm_offset (default 0).

Every JSON response wraps its payload in a top-level result key; list responses carry the X-Total-Count header with the total record count and a Link header with rel="next"/"prev"/"first"/"last" pagination URLs.

POST returns 201 with a Location header pointing at the created record; PATCH returns 200 with the updated record; requests and bodies use Accept/Content-Type: application/json; Basic authentication and OAuth 2.0 are the supported authentication schemes.
REST error responses use the envelope {"error": {"message": ..., "detail": ...}, "status": "failure"}.

Inbound REST rate limiting returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header giving the seconds to wait before retrying; the exact retry budget used by this seed (3 retries) is a local policy pinned in docs/contract.json, not a vendor value.
ServiceNow guidance for offset pagination is to pin an ORDERBY in sysparm_query so pages are drawn from a stable ordering; this seed pins ^ORDERBYsys_id on every offset-paginated query.
